response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a skilled full-stack and web developer with a strong TypeScript/Node.js background, but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role. His C++ experience is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis project — far short of the mandatory 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. He has no demonstrated experience in capital markets, fixed income, investment banking, or any related financial-product domain, and no evidence of SQL Server or Oracle proficiency. His career trajectory is firmly in web, mobile, and SaaS development, making him an unsuitable candidate for this capital-markets C++ role.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) consistently applied across multiple senior roles
Broad polyglot background with exposure to lower-level languages (C/C++, DLang) alongside modern web stacks
Demonstrated public communication skills through conference talks and multi-day technical workshops
concerns: C++ experience is minimal (~6 months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 plus a thesis project), nowhere near the required 5–10 years
Zero capital markets, fixed income, investment banking, or financial-product domain exposure across the entire career history
No evidence of Windows-platform development or SQL Server/Oracle proficiency; stack is predominantly Linux, cloud, and web technologies

key_factors.factor: No Windows or enterprise DB experience
